stories.headline: Noam Shazeer, Transformer Co-Author, Leaves Google for OpenAI as Lead for Architecture Research
stories.key_takeaway: Transformer co-author Noam Shazeer has left Google for OpenAI to lead architecture research, a marquee hire timed to OpenAI's IPO push that highlights the industry's escalating war for elite AI talent.

pull_quote.text: noam is one of the people I have most wanted to work with since the very beginning of openai. only took 10 years. i think it will be worth the wait!

data_points.label: Paid by Google in 2024 to bring Shazeer back
Tenure back at Google before leaving

stories.article_excerpt: Noam Shazeer, the engineer whose ideas sit at the foundation of nearly every modern large language model, is leaving Google.

stories.headline: SpaceX Plans to Build AI Data Centers in Orbit as Terrestrial Power Runs Short
stories.key_takeaway: SpaceX is advancing orbital AI data centers powered by space solar to escape Earth's grid and land constraints, but engineering experts warn the physics and economics remain unproven.

pull_quote.text: There is a vast difference between launching satellites and operating an industrial-scale computing infrastructure in orbit. Space is unforgiving.
pull_quote.role: Engineering professors, writing in The Conversation

stories.article_excerpt: Ten days after pulling off the largest IPO in history, SpaceX is asking investors to believe in space-based compute.

stories.headline: Epic's Unreal Engine 6 Makes Claude and Gemini Core Engine Pillars
stories.key_takeaway: Epic has made AI models Claude and Gemini a foundational pillar of Unreal Engine 6 via an open MCP layer, embedding generative AI into the industry's leading engine despite 52%+ of developers viewing the technology as harmful.

pull_quote.text: For UE6, we see LLMs, generative AI models, and tools like Claude and Codex playing a central role in helping you build content faster while maintaining the creative control you need.

stories.article_excerpt: When Epic Games walked onstage at Unreal Fest Chicago on June 17, 2026, the most consequential announcement was AI.

stories.headline: OpenAI and Anthropic File Confidential IPOs as the AI Listing Supercycle Arrives
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic and OpenAI have both confidentially filed for IPOs days apart, and with SpaceX now public, the long-anticipated AI listing supercycle worth a potential $3.5 trillion is underway.

pull_quote.text: It's important for the AI industry that these IPOs go well, and I actually think they will go well, because they're doing well.

stories.article_excerpt: In the span of eleven days, the two companies that defined the generative-AI era both told Wall Street they want to go public.

stories.headline: Google's Android 17 Ships With Gemini Omni and Lyria 3 Music Generation Built In
stories.key_takeaway: Android 17 embeds Gemini Omni, Lyria 3 and AudioLM at the OS level and exposes them to third-party developers via Android AI APIs, laying the groundwork for Google's agentic 'Gemini Intelligence' initiative.

pull_quote.text: If your prompt names a specific artist, Gemini will take this as broad creative inspiration and create a track that shares a similar style or mood.

data_points.label: Min RAM for on-device agentic features

stories.article_excerpt: Google has stopped treating AI as an app you open and started treating it as part of the operating system itself.

stories.headline: OpenRouter's Fusion Blends Multiple Models to Rival Fable 5 — at Half the Cost
stories.key_takeaway: OpenRouter's Fusion synthesizes multiple models' outputs into one response, matching or beating Fable 5 on the DRACO benchmark at roughly half the cost — arriving the day after a US export ban took Fable 5 offline.

pull_quote.text: Fable-level intelligence at half the price.

data_points.label: Gain from fusing Opus 4.8 with itself

stories.article_excerpt: The day after Anthropic's flagship Fable 5 went dark under a US export-control order, OpenRouter shipped a product.

stories.headline: Claude Code Ships Nested Subagents to Tame Long, Multi-Hour Coding Sessions
stories.key_takeaway: Claude Code now lets agents nest subagents five levels deep to preserve context across long sessions, while a parallel update blocks destructive git and infrastructure commands in auto mode unless explicitly requested.

pull_quote.text: Agents kicking off agents as a way to better manage context.

stories.article_excerpt: For most of the past year, the dirty secret of agentic coding has been the same one that has dogged LLMs: context.

stories.headline: OpenAI's Record and Replay Turns One-Off Workflows Into Reusable Codex Skills
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's new Record & Replay lets Codex watch a user perform a workflow once and convert it into a reusable, model-authored skill, repositioning Codex from coding assistant to desktop workflow-automation platform.

pull_quote.text: Record & Replay lets you demonstrate a workflow on your Mac and turn it into a reusable skill. Use it when the workflow is repetitive, depends on your preferences, or is easier to show than to describe in a prompt.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI has quietly handed Codex a new way to learn: just show it what to do, once.

stories.headline: Anthropic Study of 400,000 Sessions Finds Domain Expertise Beats Coding Skill in Claude Code
stories.key_takeaway: Across 400,000 Claude Code sessions, a user's domain expertise — not programming skill — determined how much autonomous work the model performed and how often sessions succeeded.

pull_quote.text: Coding agents are not substituting for domain expertise—the more understanding a worker brings to an agent, the more quality work the agent is able to do.
pull_quote.role: Authors of 'Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise'

data_points.label: Claude actions per prompt, expert vs novice

stories.article_excerpt: The conventional wisdom about coding assistants has been that the better a programmer you are, the more you get out.

stories.headline: AI Agents Resolve Open Math Conjectures With Proofs Checked Line-by-Line in Lean 4
stories.key_takeaway: A two-agent AI framework from Peking University's AI4Math team resolved a 2014 open problem in commutative algebra and verified the proof in roughly 19,000 lines of Lean 4 with essentially no human mathematical input.

pull_quote.text: Mathematical proofs demand complete rigor, yet even expert-written proofs may contain subtle flaws, and proofs produced by LLMs, which are prone to hallucination, are far less reliable.
pull_quote.attribution: Haocheng Ju, Guoxiong Gao, Bin Dong et al.

data_points.label: Year the resolved problem was posed

stories.article_excerpt: A pair of AI agents has resolved a mathematical question that sat open for more than a decade — and proved it formally.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind's AI Co-Mathematician Scores 48% on FrontierMath's Hardest Tier
stories.key_takeaway: DeepMind's AI co-mathematician, a stateful multi-agent workbench built around human collaboration, scored 48% on FrontierMath Tier 4, roughly quadrupling the prior best on math's hardest AI benchmark.

pull_quote.text: We introduce the AI co-mathematician, a workbench for mathematicians to interactively leverage AI agents to pursue open-ended research.
pull_quote.attribution: Daniel Zheng, Pushmeet Kohli et al.

stories.article_excerpt: Google DeepMind has built a research assistant for mathematicians that sits beside them rather than just answering.

stories.headline: KAIST's VOTP Teaches AI Human Judgment From a Handful of Preference Videos
stories.key_takeaway: KAIST's VOTP uses video foundation models and optimal transport to teach AI human preferences from a handful of videos instead of tens of thousands of human-labeled comparisons, slashing the labeling cost of preference learning.

pull_quote.text: Since VOTP can learn human judgment criteria with only a small number of videos, it is a core technology that will accelerate the era of robots making human-like judgments.
pull_quote.role: Professor, School of Electrical Engineering, KAIST

data_points.label: Robot arm used in real tabletop tests

stories.article_excerpt: Teaching a robot to act the way a person would want has long carried a hidden tax: someone has to label the data.

stories.headline: 42 State Attorneys General Launch Sweeping Investigation Into OpenAI Ahead of Its IPO
stories.key_takeaway: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general, led by New York's Letitia James, has subpoenaed OpenAI over its ads, data, treatment of minors and seniors, and model sycophancy just days after the company filed confidentially for a roughly $1 trillion IPO.

pull_quote.text: We take the concerns raised by state attorneys general seriously and intend to engage constructively with their offices.
pull_quote.role: Statement to The Wall Street Journal

data_points.label: Gap between IPO filing and subpoena

stories.article_excerpt: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general has opened a sweeping investigation into OpenAI, with subpoenas in hand.

stories.headline: China Unveils a $295 Billion Five-Year AI Infrastructure Plan
stories.key_takeaway: China's $295 billion, five-year state-directed AI infrastructure plan answers U.S. export controls by building a Huawei-anchored national compute grid, betting industrial policy can beat gatekeeping.

pull_quote.role: Co-founder, Z.ai, on Musk's Q1 2027 Fable 5-parity prediction

stories.article_excerpt: Beijing has put a number on its ambition: a $295 billion, five-year plan to fund a nationwide AI buildout.

stories.headline: The White House Executive Order That Explains the Fable 5 Ban
stories.key_takeaway: The June 2 executive order built a 'voluntary' framework for the government to vet frontier AI models; when Anthropic released Fable 5 before that framework existed, the June 12 ban produced the cooperation through enforcement instead.

pull_quote.text: This sure looks mandatory if there are going to be consequences for not doing what the government says.
pull_quote.role: Senior Fellow, Center for a New American Security

stories.article_excerpt: When the U.S. suspended foreign access to Anthropic's most powerful AI models, the order did not come out of nowhere.

stories.headline: Congress Unveils the 'Great American AI Act,' Its Most Comprehensive Federal AI Framework Yet
stories.key_takeaway: A bipartisan 269-page discussion draft would build the most comprehensive federal AI framework yet, pairing new safety and audit duties for frontier developers with a controversial three-year preemption of state AI-development laws.

pull_quote.text: This bipartisan framework is designed to meet the challenges posed by this rapidly advancing technology without smothering American innovation.

data_points.label: Revenue threshold for audited 'large frontier developer'

stories.article_excerpt: Two House lawmakers from opposite parties have produced the most ambitious attempt yet at a national AI rulebook.
